mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to corporate earnings, increasing the present value of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the conditional mean return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with 60-day forward returns showing a negative or zero mean when both RSAFS and HOUST rise concurrently over a 24-month sample.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a monthly decline in the index of industrial production indicates capacity constraints and slowing output, which forces equity valuations down and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat following INDPRO declines in late-cycle periods, or the predictive slope coefficient is statistically insignificant (t-stat < 1.96) in a regime-filtered regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect sustained aggregate demand growth, which translates into higher corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the out-of-sample R-squared of the predictive model falls below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL surpasses 220, or the conditional return distribution shows no significant left tail.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling industrial production signals labor hoarding and declining productivity, which erodes profit margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of cases, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over a 36-month horizon.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a monthly increase in housing starts indicates renewed credit availability and consumer confidence, which rapidly lifts equity prices and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following HOUST increases are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when filtered by regime classification.
